description_long: This is a small, single-engine airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a high-wing configuration, typical of general aviation aircraft like the Cessna 172. The wings are straight and mounted above the main body, providing stability and good visibility below. The airplane features a tricycle landing gear setup, with two main wheels under the wings and a single nose wheel. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with windows visible along the sides. The exterior is primarily a light gray or white color with subtle blue accents near the tail.
description: A small, high-wing, single-engine airplane with tricycle landing gear and a conventional tail, finished in light gray with blue accents.
description_view_0: Side-rear view showing the high-mounted straight wings, single propeller at the nose, and blue accent near the tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide wingspan, tricycle landing gear, and the propeller at the nose.
description_view_2: Side-rear view from the opposite side, again showing the high-wing configuration and tail details.
description_view_3: Direct rear view displaying the tail's vertical and horizontal stabilizers and the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a front propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed configuration.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with side windows, likely single or dual seat.
